column2: DUT uses the standard NSC 7-point achievement level scale: L7=80-100%
column8: L1=0-29%. The entry requirements document specifies required achievement levels per subject rather than a composite APS total. English can be met via either Home Language (HL) OR First Additional Language (FAL) at the stated level — whichever is higher applies. "A ranking system will apply" meaning exceeding minimum requirements improves admission chances. Applications are submitted via the Central Applications Office (CAO) at www.cao.ac.za (R250 application fee for SA citizens). General application closing date: 30 September 2025. Engineering and ICT programmes may remain open until 1 February 2026. Campuses: Durban (Steve Biko/ML Sultan/Ritson) and Pietermaritzburg (Indumiso).
